# IMPLEMENTATION PLAN

## Phase 1: Foundation (Day 1‑2)

| # | Task | Owner | Estimated Time |
|---|------|-------|-----------------|
| 1 | **Create repository & CI** – init Git repo, add `.gitignore`, set up GitHub Actions for lint, build, test on Linux/macOS/Windows. | Lead Dev | 2 h |
| 2 | **Select language & toolchain** – decide on Rust 1.78+, install `cargo`, `rustup`, configure `clippy` and `rustfmt`. | Lead Dev | 1 h |
| 3 | **Scaffold project** – `cargo new cli-agent-terminal-publish --bin`, add workspace layout, create `src/cli.rs`, `src/eval.rs`, `src/ui.rs` placeholders. | Dev 1 | 2 h |
| 4 | **Add core dependencies** – `clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }`, `anyhow`, `thiserror`, `log` + `env_logger`. | Dev 2 | 1 h |
| 5 | **Implement basic CLI skeleton** – global flags (`--help`, `--version`, `--interactive`), sub‑command `calc <EXPR>` for batch mode. Return a stub result. | Dev 1 | 3 h |
| 6 | **Set up testing framework** – add `dev-dependencies` (`assert_cmd`, `predicates`, `insta`) and create a simple test that runs `calc "1+1"` and checks exit code. | Dev 2 | 2 h |
| 7 | **Cross‑platform sanity check** – compile on Linux, macOS, Windows runners; verify binary runs and prints usage. | Lead Dev | 2 h |
| 8 | **Documentation bootstrap** – create `README.md` with quick start, generate `Cargo.toml` description, add `CHANGELOG.md`. | Docs | 1 h |
| **Total** | | | **14 h** |

---

## Phase 2: Core Features (Day 3‑5)

| # | Task | Owner | Estimated Time |
|---|------|-------|-----------------|
| 1 | **Expression parser & evaluator** – integrate `meval` (or implement shunting‑yard) to support: arithmetic, trig, log, constants (`π`, `e`), parentheses, variable assignment. Add custom error types. | Dev 1 | 6 h |
| 2 | **Batch mode implementation** – `calc <EXPR>` parses, evaluates, prints result with optional `--precision <N>` flag. Add `--output json|plain` flag. | Dev 1 | 3 h |
| 3 | **Interactive mode skeleton** – use `ratatui` + `crossterm` to create a full‑screen TUI with input line, output pane, and status bar. | Dev 2 | 5 h |
| 4 | **Real‑time preview** – as user types, debounce input (300 ms) and evaluate in background thread, display result in preview pane. Handle errors gracefully. | Dev 2 | 4 h |
| 5 | **Syntax highlighting** – integrate `syntect` with a custom `.sublime-syntax` for mathematical expressions; apply colours to the input line. | Dev 2 | 3 h |
| 6 | **Contextual help system** – press `F1` or `?` to open a side pane with quick reference (operators, functions, constants). Populate from a static markdown file parsed at runtime. | Dev 1 | 3 h |
| 7 | **History & clipboard** – maintain an in‑memory history (up/down arrows) and enable copying the last result with `Ctrl‑C`. Persist last 100 entries to `$XDG_DATA_HOME/cli-agent-terminal-publish/history.json`. | Dev 1 | 3 h |
| 8 | **Configuration file** – support optional `~/.config/cli-agent-terminal-publish/config.toml` for defaults (precision, theme, keybindings). Use `serde` + `toml`. | Dev 2 | 2 h |
| 9 | **Packaging scripts** – create Homebrew formula, Scoop manifest, and a simple `install.sh` that downloads the appropriate binary from GitHub releases. | Dev 1 | 2 h |
| **Total** | | | **31 h** |

---

## Phase 3: Polish & Testing (Day 6‑7)

| # | Task | Owner | Estimated Time |
|---|------|-------|-----------------|
| 1 | **Comprehensive unit tests** – cover parser edge cases, function handling, error messages, precision rounding. Aim for ≥ 90 % coverage. | QA | 4 h |
| 2 | **Integration tests** – spawn the binary in batch mode with various expressions, verify stdout/stderr, test interactive mode via `expect` scripts. | QA | 4 h |
| 3 | **Performance benchmarks** – use `criterion` to benchmark evaluation of large expressions; ensure < 5 ms on typical hardware. Optimize if needed. | Dev 2 | 3 h |
| 4 | **Accessibility review** – verify colour contrast, support for high‑contrast terminal themes, ensure all actions have keyboard shortcuts. | Docs | 2 h |
| 5 | **Documentation finalisation** – expand README with usage examples, add `MANUAL.md`, generate man page via `cargo man`. | Docs | 3 h |
| 6 | **Release pipeline** – configure GitHub Actions to build release binaries for `x86_64`/`aarch64` on Linux/macOS/Windows, sign artifacts, publish to GitHub Releases. | Lead Dev | 3 h |
| 7 | **User feedback loop** – open a beta issue template, collect early tester feedback, triage bugs. | Lead Dev | 2 h |
| 8 | **Final code audit & cleanup** – run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, format with `rustfmt`, remove dead code/comments. | All | 2 h |
| **Total** | | | **23 h** |

## File Structure (planned)

```
cli-agent-terminal-publish/
├─ .github/
│   └─ workflows/
│       └─ ci.yml                # CI/CD pipeline
├─ assets/
│   ├─ syntax/
│   │   └─ math.sublime-syntax   # syntax definition for highlighting
│   └─ help/
│       └─ reference.md          # contextual help content
├─ src/
│   ├─ main.rs                   # entry point, CLI dispatch
│   ├─ cli.rs                    # clap definitions, flag handling
│   ├─ eval/
│   │   ├─ mod.rs                # public API
│   │   ├─ parser.rs             # expression parser (meval wrapper)
│   │   └─ errors.rs             # domain errors
│   ├─ ui/
│   │   ├─ mod.rs                # interactive UI entry
│   │   ├─ app.rs                # ratatui App struct
│   │   ├─ view.rs               # layout & rendering
│   │   └─ events.rs             # input handling & debounce
│   └─ config.rs                 # TOML config loader
├─ tests/
│   ├─ batch.rs                  # batch mode tests
│   ├─ interactive.rs            # expect‑style UI tests
│   └─ parser.rs                 # unit tests for parser
├─ benches/
│   └─ eval_bench.rs             # criterion benchmarks
├─ Cargo.toml
├─ Cargo.lock
├─ README.md
├─ CHANGELOG.md
├─ MANUAL.md
└─ LICENSE
```

---

## Tech Decisions

| Area | Choice | Rationale |
|------|--------|------------|
| **Language** | **Rust 1.78+** | Produces single, static binaries; strong type safety; excellent cross‑platform tooling; high performance for numeric workloads. |
| **CLI parsing** | `clap` (v4, derive) | Declarative, auto‑generated help, sub‑command support, easy flag handling, widely used in Rust ecosystem. |
| **Expression evaluation** | `meval` (or custom shunting‑yard) | `meval` already supports scientific functions, constants, and variable assignment; fallback to custom parser if we need tighter control. |
| **Interactive UI** | `ratatui` + `crossterm` | Pure‑Rust TUI library, supports ANSI colours, mouse/keyboard events, works on Windows ConHost, PowerShell, macOS Terminal, Linux. |
| **Syntax highlighting** | `syntect` (with custom `.sublime‑syntax`) | Fast, offline highlighting; can be bundled without external assets. |
| **Configuration** | `serde` + `toml` | Human‑readable config, easy deserialization, matches XDG spec. |
| **Testing** | `assert_cmd`, `predicates`, `insta`, `criterion` | Provides binary‑level testing, snapshot testing, and reliable benchmarking. |
| **CI/CD** | GitHub Actions (ubuntu‑latest, macos‑latest, windows‑latest) | Native integration, matrix builds, artifact upload for releases. |
| **Packaging** | Homebrew formula, Scoop manifest, `install.sh` script | Covers the three major OS ecosystems; users can install with a single command. |

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|------------|
| **Parser edge‑cases** (e.g., ambiguous unary minus, implicit multiplication) | Incorrect results / crashes | Medium | Write exhaustive unit tests; adopt a well‑tested library (`meval`) before considering custom logic. |
| **Windows terminal compatibility** (ANSI codes, Unicode) | Poor UX for a large user base | Medium | Use `crossterm` which abstracts platform differences; test on PowerShell, CMD, Windows Terminal via CI. |
| **Performance regression** with very large expressions | Slow interactive preview, UI lag | Low | Benchmark with `criterion`; implement lazy evaluation and debounce; fallback to batch‑mode evaluation if needed. |
| **Binary size** (static linking may inflate > 10 MB) | Distribution friction | Low | Strip symbols (`cargo build --release --target ... -Z strip`), enable `lto = true` in `Cargo.toml`. |
| **Dependency breakage** (major version bump of `clap` or `ratatui`) | Build failures | Low | Pin exact versions, use Cargo’s `cargo update -p` policy, add CI test on nightly channel. |
| **User configuration conflicts** (invalid TOML) | Crashes on start | Low | Validate config on load, provide clear error messages, fall back to defaults. |
| **Security (code injection via expression)** | Potential arbitrary code execution | Low | Use a sandboxed evaluator (`meval` does not expose Rust code execution); never `eval` raw Rust. |
| **Release pipeline failures** (signing, artifact upload) | Delayed releases | Low | Automate with GitHub Actions, include a manual fallback script, test pipeline on a dummy tag before production. |
